summary: The vascular inflammatory response starts immediately from mast cells releasing histamines and bradykins followed shortly after by minor damage due to environmental factors; then comes an extended period where white blood cells cause tissue harm over time before finally enduring significant but slower onset injuries caused by bacteria. \(\boxed{\text{B}}\)
